figures.caption: Figure 3. Thermal-Det zero-shot performance across various benchmarks compared with other OVDs with Swin-T backbone.

Figure 2. Thermal-Det combines a large language model (LLM) with a dual-stream RGB–thermal detector for zero-shot thermal object detection. The RGB branch acts as a frozen teacher, while the thermal branch learns transferable semantics through feature-, box-, and confidence-level distillation losses (LKD-sem,LKD-box,LKD-conf). The LLM generates scene- and object-level captions from visual proposals and text tokens, guided by a caption loss (Lcap) with gating-controlled cross-attention. The LLM is discarded at inference.
Table 2. Comparison of detection performance on FLIR-Aligned and CAMEL datasets. Bold rows indicate our model’s performance relative to each baseline.
Table 1. Zero-shot detection results on thermal datasets FLIR-Aligned, FLIR-V2, camel, and Utokyo.
